mnemonics: FIST - Four key properties of a good test: Fast, Isolated, Small/Simple, Transparent
keyPoints: Unit tests verify the correctness of individual components in isolation.
Integration tests check the interactions between multiple integrated components.
Mocks and stubs are used to simulate the behavior of complex dependencies that are not under test.
commonMistakes: Testing the mock instead of the actual component under test.
Writing tests that are not isolated and depend on the state from previous tests.
Creating overly complex test setups that are difficult to understand and maintain.
